name: benwu232
body:
# Summary

Name: Wu, Bin(Ben Wu).

Professional engineer & creative researcher.

Interests: Machine Learning, Pattern Recognition and Human-Computer Interaction.

# Contact

Email: benwu232@gmail.com

Facebook, Google+, Twitter, Weibo: *benwu232*

# Projects
  

## TapMagicPlayer

TapMagic is an experimental technology of using tapping to control hand-held devices. 

TapMagicPlayer is a try of using TapMagic in a real application, which brings a natural and elegant way to control while playing videos. While playing, tap the left side of the device to step forward for a few seconds; tap the right side to step backward for a few seconds; double-tap the back side to play/pause the video. A demo is [here](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uMJtmbJmHO8). Please try it [here](https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/tapmagicplayer/id625703421?ls=1&mt=8) in Apple's App Store or use the 2D-code ![TapMagicPlayer](tmp_itunes_2dcode.png)

I'm sure that TapMagic has a broad range of uses. If you have any idea or suggestion, please let me know or disscuss in the [group](https://groups.google.com/forum/?fromgroups#!forum/tapmagic).
   

## Quantum Phantom
QP is a concept prototype of a new technology, which turns a webcam into a non-touch pointing device that
can directly control the objects on screen, and even draw or write on it. It is based on computer vision,
machine learning and pattern recognition.

The project is here:
https://github.com/benwu232/quantum_phantom

The demo videos are on 

[YouTube1](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LUyql0SVobc)

[YouTube2](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ExE5m6BjnV0)

or on 

[youku1](http://v.youku.com/v_show/id_XMjkyMzg5NzQ4.html)

[youku2](http://v.youku.com/v_show/id_XMjk5NTg5MTQ0.html)

and the relevant reports are 

[Link1](http://www.engadget.com/2011/08/12/quantum-phantom-prototype-lets-you-control-your-computer-screen/)

[Link2](http://www.geek.com/articles/gadgets/quantum-phantom-styles-itself-after-tony-starks-computer-20110812/)
   

## AGRE
AGRE(Accelerometer based Gesture Recognition Engine) is an experimental project, which analyzes the motion data sampled by Wiimote to recognize gestures of digits and some simple symbols.

The project is here:
https://github.com/benwu232/agre

The demo video is on 

[YouTube](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YQqPQQ5sEQY&feature=related) 

or 

[youku](http://v.youku.com/v_show/id_XMjkyMzc4Mzg0.html)
